etymology.description: 下(下) (xià, "below") + 午(午) (wǔ, "noon") = the period after noon
etymology.interpretation: 下(下) (xià, "below") indicates a position after or below in sequence, and 午(午) (wǔ, "noon") refers to the middle of the day. Thus, in 上午(上午), their combination suggests the 'afternoon,' being the time below or after noon.

examples.chinese: 下午(下午)，我和朋友去看电影(我和朋友去看電影)。
examples.pinyin: Xiàwǔ, wǒ hé péngyǒu qù kàn diànyǐng.
examples.english: In the afternoon, my friend and I go to watch a movie.

examples.chinese: 每天的下午(每天的下午)，他都去公园散步(他都去公園散步)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān de xiàwǔ, tā dōu qù gōngyuán sànbù.
examples.english: Every afternoon, he goes to the park for a walk.

examples.chinese: 我们将在下午开会(我們將在下午開會)。
examples.english: We will have a meeting in the afternoon.
